channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Priceless 2,500-year-old golden helmet returned to Romania after Dutch museum raid
item.description: The ornate Cotofenesti helmet, which was stolen in January 2025 while on loan to a Dutch museum, was recovered last month.
item.content:encoded:
The ornate Cotofenesti helmet, which was stolen in January 2025 while on loan to a Dutch museum, was recovered last month.

(Image credit: Andreea Alexandru)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Mexico to beef up security at tourist sites after shooting at pyramids
item.description: Mexico's government is boosting security at tourist sites in preparation for the World Cup after a man opened fire at the Teotihuacan pyramids.
item.content:encoded: Mexico's government is boosting security at tourist sites in preparation for the World Cup after a man opened fire at the Teotihuacan pyramids.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: As Pakistan seeks to broker U.S.–Iran peace, citizens watch for gains at home
item.description: As Pakistan works to broker peace between the U.S. and Iran, many Pakistanis are watching closely to see whether the diplomatic boost will translate into improvements at home.
item.content:encoded: As Pakistan works to broker peace between the U.S. and Iran, many Pakistanis are watching closely to see whether the diplomatic boost will translate into improvements at home.
item.dc:creator: Betsy Joles

item.title: What would happen next if Iran agreed to surrender its highly enriched uranium
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Scott Roecker of the Nuclear Threat Initiative about how the U.S. might go about moving highly enriched uranium out of Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Scott Roecker of the Nuclear Threat Initiative about how the U.S. might go about moving highly enriched uranium out of Iran.
item.dc:creator: Scott Detrow

item.title: Trump extends U.S. ceasefire with Iran at Pakistan's request
item.description: The extension was announced just hours before it was set to expire. The president did not provide details on how long the new ceasefire extension will last.
item.content:encoded:
The extension was announced just hours before it was set to expire. The president did not provide details on how long the new ceasefire extension will last.

(Image credit: Aamir Qureshi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: U.S.-Iran ceasefire nears its end. And, Fed chair nominee faces tough hearing
item.description: The ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is set to expire tomorrow. Peace talks between the countries remain uncertain. And, Trump's pick to lead the Federal Reserve faces a tough confirmation hearing today.
item.content:encoded:
The ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is set to expire tomorrow. Peace talks between the countries remain uncertain. And, Trump's pick to lead the Federal Reserve faces a tough confirmation hearing today.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran ends soon
item.description: The ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran ends soon. President Trump says a U.S. delegation is going to Pakistan for talks, but Iran hasn't confirmed their attendance.
item.content:encoded: The ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran ends soon. President Trump says a U.S. delegation is going to Pakistan for talks, but Iran hasn't confirmed their attendance.
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf

item.title: Japan approves removing ban on lethal weapons exports
item.description: Japan endorsed scrapping a ban on lethal weapons exports, a significant change of its postwar pacifist policy, as the country seeks to build up its arms industry.
item.content:encoded: Japan endorsed scrapping a ban on lethal weapons exports, a significant change of its postwar pacifist policy, as the country seeks to build up its arms industry.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kuhn

item.title: Gulf aluminum shipments stuck as Strait of Hormuz blockades put a strain on supply
item.description: A U.S. blockade and conflict in the Strait of Hormuz have stranded Gulf aluminum shipments and driven prices to a four-year high, exposing global supply vulnerabilities and deepening trade strain.
item.content:encoded: A U.S. blockade and conflict in the Strait of Hormuz have stranded Gulf aluminum shipments and driven prices to a four-year high, exposing global supply vulnerabilities and deepening trade strain.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Northam

item.title: Gunman at Mexican pyramids carried materials related to Columbine massacre
item.description: The gunman who killed a Canadian tourist and left at least 13 people injured at Mexico's iconic Teotihuacan pyramids carried materials that were apparently related to the deadly 1999 high school shooting, authorities said Tuesday.
item.content:encoded:
The gunman who killed a Canadian tourist and left at least 13 people injured at Mexico's iconic Teotihuacan pyramids carried materials that were apparently related to the deadly 1999 high school shooting, authorities said Tuesday.

(Image credit: Eduardo Verdugo)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
